purpose: Curated static US-equity stress windows for Practical Validation. These windows are validation presets, not trading signals or investment recommendations.
coverage_note: The list focuses on widely observed US equity market shock periods from 2000 onward. It is intentionally deterministic and should be reviewed when new major stress events occur.

date_policy.monthly_data_fallback: If the portfolio curve is monthly only, use the full calendar months touched by the window.
date_policy.not_covered_status: NOT_RUN: period does not cover stress window

windows.label: Dot-com bust / early-2000s bear market
windows.notes: Long technology-led bear market around the 2001 recession. Useful for testing equity and growth concentration.

windows.label: 9/11 market closure and reopening stress
windows.notes: US equity markets reopened on 2001-09-17 after the September 11 attacks. Use daily data when available.

windows.label: Global financial crisis bear market
windows.notes: Equity peak-to-trough style window around the Great Recession and credit crisis.

windows.label: Lehman / acute liquidity crisis
windows.notes: Acute post-Lehman stress sub-window inside the broader financial crisis.

windows.label: May 2010 Flash Crash
windows.notes: One-day market structure event. Monthly-only curves should mark this as NOT_RUN or use May 2010 as a coarse fallback with clear limitation.

windows.label: US debt-ceiling downgrade / eurozone stress
windows.notes: US debt-ceiling, rating downgrade, and eurozone stress correction window.

windows.label: China devaluation / global growth scare
windows.notes: Global risk-off period after China renminbi devaluation and growth concerns.

windows.label: February 2018 volatility shock
windows.notes: Short-volatility unwind / VIX spike period. Useful for leveraged and volatility-sensitive exposure checks.

windows.label: Q4 2018 Fed / trade / growth scare
windows.notes: Sharp equity declines amid growth concerns, trade tensions, and Fed communication risk.

windows.label: COVID crash
windows.notes: Fast pandemic-driven equity and liquidity shock. Monthly fallback can use 2020-02 through 2020-04.

windows.label: 2022 inflation / rate shock
windows.notes: Rising-rate / inflation shock period that stressed both growth equities and duration-sensitive assets.

windows.label: March 2023 banking stress
windows.notes: US regional banking stress around SVB / Signature failures and related market volatility.

windows.label: August 2024 carry-trade unwind / volatility spike
windows.notes: Early-August market turbulence associated with macro surprise and leveraged carry-trade unwind.

windows.label: April 2025 tariff shock
windows.notes: Sharp equity selloff around newly announced US tariff policy. Review this preset as tariff policy dates evolve.
